clusters.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in airstrike, Trump says
clusters.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

clusters.headline: Mother finds body of missing son two days after Kenya's Ebola quarantine centre protests
clusters.summary: Sylvester Muigai Ndung'u was in Nanyuki to fetch a school uniform on Tuesday, his mother says.

clusters.headline: England training equipment stolen ahead of squad’s arrival in Kansas City
clusters.summary: Local police say an investigation is under way over the possible theft of equipment which was arriving in Kansas City.

clusters.headline: Norway: International law is worth defending, even when allies break it
clusters.summary:
Norway: International law is worth defending, even when allies break it

    
    

        
    
                International law should be defended even when the country's closest allies are the ones breaking it, said Norway's deputy foreign minister as Oslo reiterated its cond

clusters.headline: South Africa trolled by African fans in wake of World Cup loss
clusters.summary: The taunting reflects anger from parts of the continent over reports of xenophobia in South Africa.

clusters.headline: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap population at 10 million
clusters.summary: The right-wing Swiss People's Party calls the plan a "sustainability initiative",  but opponents say it is a recipe for chaos.

clusters.headline: Senegal: Institut Pasteur de Dakar Sequences the First Human Whole Genomes in the Country and Launches an Early Cancer Detection Platform
clusters.summary: [Institut Pasteur de Dakar] First 40 human whole genomes sequenced using Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies platforms Institut Pasteur de Dakar launches clinical laboratory and bioinformatics platform, enabling testing for predisposition to early-onset breast cancer in Senegal and other rare

clusters.headline: Fast-track border checks, return centers, 'mandatory solidarity': What the EU’s new asylum rules could change — and what they may not.
clusters.summary: Germany aims to further reduce the number of new asylum seekers. The reformed EU asylum law (CEAS) came into effect on Friday, but experts say it is unlikely to achieve all it sets out to do.

clusters.headline: PSA pays fine over worker's death at Keppel Terminal in 2017, but appeals guilty verdict and sentence
clusters.summary: The deceased, 29-year-old technical specialist Lee Swee Loong, was crushed between a rope drum and a trolley platform while carrying out maintenance checks on a crane.

clusters.headline: 'Never wanted a snap state election' in Negeri Sembilan, but no choice given BN's actions: Anwar
clusters.summary: Malaysia Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim was speaking on Friday (Jun 12) night at the launch of his coalition's election campaign for the Negeri Sembilan state polls.

clusters.headline: Taiwan opposition leader seeks to ease Washington's concerns over China stance during US trip
clusters.summary: Cheng Li-wun, chairwoman of the Kuomintang (KMT), has been on a two-week US trip following her April visit to China, where she met President Xi Jinping.

clusters.headline: Married at First Sight Australia stars not told partners had drug and violence convictions
clusters.summary: Stars of the hit TV show say they did not know their on-screen husbands had previous convictions.
